english: I would like a full price ticket.
choicesEnglish: I would like a full price ticket.
I am looking for a free table.

correctAnswerEnglish: I would like a full price ticket.

feedback.correctItalian: Ben fatto! Questa è la richiesta standard alla biglietteria.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo un biglietto a prezzo pieno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! This is the standard request at the ticket office.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for a full price ticket.

english: Is there a discount for students?
choicesItalian: A che ora parte il treno?
choicesEnglish: Is there a discount for students?
What time does the train leave?
I would like an ice cream.
correctAnswerEnglish: Is there a discount for students?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere sconti è sempre una buona idea.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo se gli studenti pagano meno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking for discounts is always a good idea.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking if students pay less.

choicesEnglish: Is the ticket reduced for children?
correctAnswerItalian: Il biglietto è ridotto per i bambini?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Molti musei hanno prezzi speciali per i più piccoli.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo del prezzo per bambini.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Many museums have special prices for little ones.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking about the price for children.

english: Do you have an audio guide in English?
choicesEnglish: Do you have an audio guide in English?
Do you have a free room?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you have an audio guide in English?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! L'audioguida aiuta a capire meglio la mostra.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo del dispositivo audio in lingua inglese.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The audio guide helps you understand the exhibition better.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for the audio device in English.

english: How much does the audio guide cost?
choicesItalian: A che ora chiude il bar?
choicesEnglish: How much does the audio guide cost?
What time does the bar close?
correctAnswerEnglish: How much does the audio guide cost?

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Sapere il costo extra è utile.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo il prezzo del noleggio dell'audioguida.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Knowing the extra cost is useful.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking the price for the audio guide rental.

english: Where does the exhibition start?
correctAnswerEnglish: Where does the exhibition start?

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Chiedi il punto di partenza del percorso.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchi l'ingresso del percorso espositivo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! You are asking for the starting point of the route.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are looking for the entrance to the exhibition route.

english: What is the recommended route?
correctAnswerEnglish: What is the recommended route?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Seguire il percorso consigliato aiuta a non perdere nulla.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo quale strada conviene seguire.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Following the recommended route helps you not to miss anything.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking which path is best to follow.

english: Are photos allowed?
correctAnswerEnglish: Are photos allowed?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! È sempre meglio chiedere prima di scattare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo il permesso di fotografare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It's always better to ask before taking photos.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for permission to take photos.

english: It is forbidden to use the flash.
choicesEnglish: It is forbidden to use the flash.
correctAnswerEnglish: It is forbidden to use the flash.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! La luce del flash può danneggiare i colori antichi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, questa è la regola contro l'uso del flash.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Flash light can damage antique colors.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, this is the rule against using flash.

english: Do not touch the artworks.
choicesEnglish: Do not climb on the chairs.
correctAnswerEnglish: Do not touch the artworks.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! È una regola fondamentale di ogni museo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai dicendo di non mettere le mani sulle opere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! It is a fundamental rule of every museum.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are saying not to put your hands on the works.

english: Where is the cloakroom?
correctAnswerEnglish: Where is the cloakroom?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Lì puoi lasciare giacche e borse pesanti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchi il posto per depositare i vestiti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! There you can leave jackets and heavy bags.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are looking for the place to deposit clothes.

english: Do I have to leave my backpack here?
choicesEnglish: Do I have to leave my backpack here?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do I have to leave my backpack here?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Molte gallerie non permettono zaini ingombranti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se l'obbligo di deposito vale per lo zaino.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Many galleries do not allow bulky backpacks.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking if the storage obligation applies to the backpack.

english: Is there a catalog of the exhibition?
choicesEnglish: Is there a catalog of the exhibition?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is there a catalog of the exhibition?

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Per ricordare le opere visitate.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se esiste il libro della mostra.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! To remember the works visited.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking if the exhibition book exists.

english: Where is the bookshop located?
correctAnswerEnglish: Where is the bookshop located?

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Di solito è alla fine del percorso.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchi il negozio del museo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Usually it's at the end of the route.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for the museum shop.

correctAnswerEnglish: Where is the nearest bathroom?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'informazione pratica sempre utile.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchi i servizi igienici.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Practical information that is always useful.

english: This painting is beautiful.
correctAnswerEnglish: This painting is beautiful.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Esprimere apprezzamento per l'arte.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai dicendo che il dipinto ti piace molto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Expressing appreciation for art.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are saying that you like the painting very much.

english: I really like the use of color.

correctAnswerEnglish: It is a very interesting work.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Sapere l'epoca aiuta a contestualizzare.

feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Knowing the era helps to contextualize.

correctAnswerEnglish: Is it contemporary or classical art?
